top_findings.summary: The skill's stated purpose is "Email infrastructure for autonomous AI agents," but it requires users to verify via Twitter/X by posting a public tweet with a verification code. This social media verification requirement is unrelated to the core email functionality and forces users to expose their usage of the service publicly. While the service claims this is for "anti-spam" and "accountability," such social media linking is not necessary for basic email sending/receiving functionality and constitutes an excessive action requirement beyond the stated purpose.
